text: This chapter forms a love-song which has no special reference to any particular situation; they may be understood as words addressed to the village girl by her lover, and heard either literally or in the imagination.

‘My queen that shall be’; literally, ‘thou shalt be crowned’. The Hebrew text has simply ‘Look down’, or perhaps, ‘Make thy way down’. It is difficult to see why the various heights of the Lebanon range should be mentioned here; unless, indeed, we may suppose that the house called ‘the Forest of Lebanon’ (III Kg. 7.2 and elsewhere) had its different parts or rooms named after these peaks.
‘Incense-tree’; the Latin version here transliterates, ‘trees of Lebanon’, instead of translating the second noun.
